meetings.committee: Children & Young People Overview and Scrutiny Committee

extracted_documents.title: Children and Young People Overview and Scrutiny Committee - Youth Justice Report
extracted_documents.url: https://democracy.blackburn.gov.uk/documents/s32845/Children and Young People Overview and Scrutiny Committee - Youth Justice Report.pdf

motions.title: Overview of the Youth Justice Service
motions.proposer: Alex Orwin (Service Manager, YJS) and Imran Akuji (Assistant Director)
motions.full_text: The committee is asked to note the work undertaken by the Youth Justice Service (YJS) in readiness for inspection by His Majesty's Inspectorate of Probation, and to note details of the recent youth justice reforms.
motions.opposition_strategy: Opposition will likely seek to probe the long-term financial sustainability of the service, particularly given previous discussions on non-reoccurring grant funding for Children's Social Care Reforms. They may also question the effectiveness of multi-agency partnership working, or attempt to link YJS performance to broader Children's Services challenges.
motions.ruling_group_response: Emphasise the 'Good' rating with 'Outstanding' features from the October 2022 inspection and the continuous audit activity confirming the service is 'on track' for the next inspection. Highlight the comprehensive multi-agency team and the success of the prevention service. Stress the Council's commitment to early intervention and safeguarding. For funding, reiterate the current grant funding until 2029 and the ongoing work to secure future resources, leveraging the efficiency of the integrated model.
key_data_points: YJS achieved a 'Good' rating with several 'Outstanding' features in October 2022 inspection
Continuous audit activity suggests the service is 'on track' for future inspection

Multi-agency team includes Mental Health Nurse, Public Health Community Nurse, Drug and Alcohol Substance Misuse Worker, Speech and Language Therapist, Careers Advisors, Probation Officer, Police Officer, Community Reparation Co-ordinator
Grant funding for Children's Social Care Reforms (relevant context for funding questions) lasts until 2029

questions.questioner: Ehsan Raja (Labour) or other opposition member
questions.question_text: Given the non-reoccurring nature of grant funding mentioned in the Children's Social Care Reforms, what assurances can be given about the long-term financial stability and resourcing of the Youth Justice Service beyond 2029?
recommended_answer_points: Reiterate that current grant funding is secured until 2029.
Highlight the efficiency and effectiveness of the existing multi-agency model, which optimises resource use.
Confirm ongoing strategic planning and discussions with central government and partners to secure future funding and ensure service continuity.
Emphasise the Council's commitment to prioritising youth justice and safeguarding.
questions.trap_explanation: This question seeks to expose potential future funding gaps or instability, linking the YJS report to previous concerns raised about Children's Social Care Reforms funding.

questions.questioner: Any member, potentially a supportive one to show engagement
questions.question_text: The minutes from the last meeting noted the absence of Youth Forum representatives. How is the Youth Justice Service ensuring active participation and engagement from young people, particularly given 'Participation' is a current priority?
recommended_answer_points: Acknowledge the previous absence was due to the Youth MP election results.
Reiterate 'Participation' as a key priority for the YJS.
Outline specific mechanisms for youth engagement within the YJS, such as involvement in service design or feedback processes.
Confirm commitment to ensuring Youth Forum representatives are present at future relevant meetings.
questions.trap_explanation: This is a relatively benign question, but ensures accountability for youth engagement.

questions.question_text: The report mentions a 'Good' rating with 'Outstanding' features in October 2022. Could officers elaborate on the specific developments and enhancements made to the service since then, particularly regarding victim services and prevention provision?
recommended_answer_points: Detail the development of service to victims, which is a new priority in the inspection framework.
Explain the extension of the Prevention provision, supporting children at risk of criminality.
Highlight the introduction of a Speech and Language Therapist to the team as an enhancement.
Mention continuous audit activity confirming positive progress.
questions.trap_explanation: This question is likely supportive, allowing the ruling group to showcase positive developments and preparedness.

tactical_scenarios.scenario: Opposition attempts to broaden the discussion beyond the Youth Justice Service report to wider Children's Services issues, such as overall budget pressures, staffing levels, or previous Ofsted concerns (as per the work programme).
tactical_scenarios.counter: The Chair should politely but firmly steer the discussion back to the specific Youth Justice Service report. Officers should be prepared to acknowledge the wider context briefly but pivot quickly back to the positive performance and specific remit of the YJS. Executive Member Jullie Gunn can reinforce the focus on the YJS's distinct achievements.
tactical_scenarios.standing_order: Chair's discretion on relevance of debate to agenda item.

tactical_scenarios.scenario: Low attendance from elected members, similar to the previous meeting, leading to a perception of lack of engagement or importance.
tactical_scenarios.counter: The Vice-Chair, Councillor Brian Taylor, should acknowledge the importance of the committee's work and encourage active participation from those present. Officers should proceed with a comprehensive presentation regardless, demonstrating professionalism and dedication. The Executive Member should highlight the significance of the YJS's work for the borough.

time_management.recommended_time_saving: No specific time saving measures are critical for this meeting, as the agenda appears light. Focus on thorough discussion of the Youth Justice report.

speaker_allocation.agenda_item: Overview of the Youth Justice Service

speakers.name: Alex Orwin
key_points: Provide an overview of the YJS and its statutory basis.
Highlight the multi-agency composition and holistic provision.
Detail the prevention service and its impact.
Emphasise preparedness for HMIP inspection and the 'Good' rating from 2022.

speakers.name: Imran Akuji
key_points: Reinforce key messages from Alex Orwin.
Address any specific questions regarding strategic oversight or partnership working.

speakers.name: Jullie Gunn
key_points: Provide political context and support for the YJS's work.
Emphasise the Council's commitment to youth justice and safeguarding.
Praise the officers and team for their positive performance and preparedness.

speakers.name: Ehsan Raja
key_points: Probe funding sustainability and future resource allocation.
Potentially link YJS to wider Children's Services challenges.

speakers.name: Salma Patel
key_points: Ask supportive questions about specific achievements or improvements (e.g., victim services, Speech and Language Therapist).
Reinforce positive aspects of the report.
